questions.question: What does the 'break' statement do inside a loop?
choices: Skips the rest of the current iteration and continues to the next
Exits the loop immediately, skipping any else clause
Pauses the loop until input is received
questions.explanation: break immediately exits the nearest enclosing loop. If the loop has an else clause, the else is skipped when break is used. Execution continues with the first statement after the loop.

questions.question: The built-in function ___ generates a sequence of integers commonly used with for loops.
questions.explanation: range(start, stop, step) generates integers from start up to (but not including) stop. range(5) produces 0, 1, 2, 3, 4. It is memory-efficient because it generates values on demand.

questions.question: When does the else clause of a while or for loop execute?
choices: When the loop body raises an exception
After every iteration of the loop
When the loop finishes normally without hitting a break
Only when the loop body is empty
questions.explanation: The else clause on a loop runs only if the loop completes without encountering a break statement. If break is executed, the else is skipped. This is useful for search loops.

questions.question: What does the 'continue' statement do inside a loop?
choices: Restarts the loop from the beginning
Skips the rest of the current iteration and jumps to the next iteration
questions.explanation: continue skips the remaining statements in the current iteration and jumps back to the loop header for the next iteration. The loop itself does not terminate.

questions.question: A ___ loop repeats as long as its test condition is true.
questions.explanation: A while loop tests its condition before each iteration. The body executes repeatedly as long as the condition is truthy. When the condition becomes falsy, the loop ends (and the else clause runs, if present).

questions.question: What does for x in [1, 2, 3]: print(x) do?
choices: Prints each element on a separate line: 1, then 2, then 3
Raises an error because x is not defined
questions.explanation: The for loop iterates over each item in the list, assigning it to x one at a time. print(x) is called for each iteration, printing 1, 2, and 3 on separate lines.

questions.question: What does range(2, 10, 3) produce?
questions.explanation: range(2, 10, 3) starts at 2 and increments by 3, stopping before 10. The values are 2, 5, 8. The next value (11) would exceed the stop value, so it is not included.

questions.question: An infinite loop can be written as while ___, which runs forever unless a break statement is encountered.
questions.explanation: while True creates an infinite loop because the condition is always truthy. These loops are useful when you need to break out based on complex conditions tested inside the loop body.
